review_body_text: It is a very good company to start your career with great salary package for fresh graduates. I learnt a lot during my time in CIMB, however the KPI target just keep getting higher and you need to work harder to achieve the KPI.

With a fast-moving performance-based work culture, I was trained to work smart, fast and deliver work accurately. Try to test your limit in this environment of long working hours and extensive work load. If you adapt to it, you can excel in many workplaces.
the good place to enhance skill in ur career in financial institutions . if ur fresh graduate just try to gain experience . just give ur base on your job
High turnover rate and role was not as described. Ended up working 12 hours most days and weekends too. Benefits were below average. But bonus is pretty good. around 6 months
Mon - Fri Friendly environment but the workload will be more than expected due to manpower shortages. The hardest part will working hours after office hours and will always stayed late
Benefit: - Medical for self & family. Free hospitalisation. - Good friendship What you learned: - Good Communication The Hardest Part: - KPI & Do sales since slow walk in at the branch
It can be hectic at one point but productive. Not many incentive for protege. Would love to see how would they expand and attract youngster into their company

Position that I'm holding is very multitasking and need to carry to target as well . For the benefits it's tip top . But for the target that we need to carry tgter with incentive scheme need to revise

review_title: Bad working culture at Finance and Risk division
review_body_text: very political culture.. some people do not want to work but want to get credit of other peoples' works. However, staff benefits are generally better than other banks.

My time at CIMB has unfortunately been less than satisfactory. While there are some positives, such as a supportive team and a convenient location, the overall experience has been marred by significant shortcomings. One of the most glaring issues is the lack of effective management. There seems to be a disconnect between upper management's vision and the day-to-day operations, leading to confusion and a sense of aimlessness among employees. This has resulted in inefficiencies and missed opportunities for improvement. Additionally, the work-life balance at CIMB leaves much to be desired. The workload can be overwhelming, often requiring employees to work long hours with little acknowledgment or compensation. This imbalance has taken a toll on morale and productivity. Furthermore, opportunities for career development are scarce. There is a noticeable absence of structured programs or support for advancing one's skills and career within the company. In conclusion, while there are some positive aspects to working at CIMB, such as a supportive team environment and convenient location, the overall experience has been subpar due to ineffective management, poor work-life balance, and limited career development opportunities. Prospective employees should carefully weigh these factors before considering employment here..

Very restricted working environment. Need to be pro in multi tasking as need to handle multi systems. Need to be highly motivated and prepare multiple report in advance by guessing the report may request by management.
pros_text: Banking industries and product details. Surrounded by senior managers.
cons_text: Work Load, old systems, difficult to get permanent.

review_body_text: As a protege, the workload is quite ok as it can definitely give you a work-life balance. However, for my department, it is actually quite boring as all you do is data entry for the whole day, the learning curve is quite limited, you wont learn much if you fulfill the contract. CIMB is actually quite a manual-based company (attendance have to sign on paper). the workers are mostly boomers, which is normal since that is how local bank is, lots of boomers
Overall, the internship was rewarding, offering significant learning and development opportunities. For future interns: be proactive, network actively, and stay organized to maximize the experience.
Overall ok but need to be patients handling customer. Colleagues nice and helpful Got shift allowance Be independent and quick learn in order to settle customer inquiries

what I enjoy while working in this company is being able to learn something new such as being able to communicate with a large number of customers because I am an introvert in talking to strangers and gaining experience in managing tasks or customers with a large number and making decisions quickly and accurately.
Ok lah, so far so good lol Can’t say too hard also can’t say easily All overall is medium only, don’t put too high expectations on banking line industry
Fun and enjoy working here as all the seniors all really helpful... As i had experienced in sale department form previous employment, it actually help me a lot

review_title: Training is provided which is help in jobscope.
review_body_text: Training is provided which is help a lot in job scope. Environment is okay, surrounded by coworker that help in so many thing. Salary is low compare to other banking industry but still okay

review_title: Fun people, company guidelines are ever changing
review_body_text: In the spend of 6 months, the company had change its guidelines 4 times which effect the sales department heavily. The company doesn't care for the sales people period.
Good place to start as a first time official job. Learn to find ways in achievement. Incentive for sale staff is average. Location is strategic. Huge market.
